scope_note: QA Orbit No-3-Divisor Overclaim Cert family [278]. Primary source for the Pisano-period framing: Wall, D. D. (1960), Fibonacci series modulo m, American Mathematical Monthly 67(6), 525-532. DOI: 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for every tested modulus m with 3 not divides m and m >= 7 excluding m = 8, the canonical period-based orbit_family(b, e, m) finds zero period-8 satellite states while the algebraic divisor shortcut orbit_family_divisor_shortcut(b, e, m) over-claims exactly 9 false satellites. The 9 over-claimed pairs are exactly the 3 by 3 grid {(a*(m//3), b*(m//3)) : a, b in {1, 2, 3}}, which has 9 distinct pairs disjoint from the singularity (m, m) when 3 does not divide m and m >= 7. Causal scope is no_3_divisor, not 5_factor: the original investigation entered through the 5|m intersection while certifying [277], but the empirical sweep of 25 tested moduli (5|m, 3 not divides m, m up to 250) plus a separate proof pass on non-5-multiple 3 not divides m moduli {7, 11, 13, 14, 16, 17, 19, 22, 23, 26, 28, 29, ...} confirmed identical 9-overclaim behavior — 5-factor is incidental. The boundary exception m = 8 is excluded from v1 because m // 3 = 2 yields a 4 by 4 grid containing 16 multiples of 2 in {1..8} including the singularity, so the shortcut overclaim is 15 not 9. Cert does NOT modify qa_orbit_rules.py (canonical replacement locked at commit e7b2af0), does NOT cover the m = 8 boundary case in v1 (separate v2 fixture or follow-up cert), does NOT cover degenerate moduli m in {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} where m // 3 produces all-pair classification, does NOT formalize the full Pisano-period structure of qa_step under the A1 correction. Lineage: surfaced during cert [277] adjacent-regime investigation 2026-05-08; design draft docs/specs/QA_ORBIT_5_FACTOR_NO_3_OVERCLAIM_CERT_DRAFT.md (commit 73916f8) explicitly broadened scope from 5|m sub-family to no_3_divisor family. Theorem NT compliance: integer arithmetic on (b, e, m); validator computes orbit_family and orbit_family_divisor_shortcut deterministically. Checks NO3_1/NO3_2/NO3_3/NO3_4/SRC/F; 8 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok.
